task_completion.description: Open the specified rocket design file in OpenRocket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket application is open and the title bar or file tree indicates that 'stability_check.ork' is loaded.

task_completion.description: Access the fin configuration to address the stability issue.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The fin configuration dialog box is open in OpenRocket, showing parameters like 'Height', 'Root chord', etc., for the trapezoidal fins.

task_completion.description: Increase the fin height to make the rocket aerodynamically stable.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The fin height parameter in the configuration dialog is set to at least 5.0 cm (0.05 m), OR the visual rocket diagram shows significantly larger fins than the initial tiny ones. The stability margin indicator (usually in the top right corner) should display a positive, stable value (e.g., > 1.0 cal).

task_completion.description: Run a flight simulation to verify the new stable design.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Flight simulations' tab is active and shows a simulation run with an 'Up to date' status or a green checkmark, indicating the simulation was successfully run with the updated design.

task_completion.description: Create a stability analysis report in the specified exports directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window is open showing the contents of a file named 'stability_report.txt' located in '/home/ga/Documents/exports/', containing notes or data about the rocket's stability.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket graphical interface to modify the rocket design.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the OpenRocket GUI (dialog boxes, simulation tabs) rather than just editing the .ork XML file directly in a text editor.

integrity.description: The flight simulation was actually executed and produced real data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The simulation tab displays realistic flight metrics (e.g., apogee, max velocity, flight time) rather than being empty or showing errors.

integrity.description: The stability report contains actual analysis rather than a hardcoded bypass string.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in 'stability_report.txt' includes specific details about the stability margin, fin adjustments, or simulation results, rather than just a generic 'rocket is stable' sentence.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is '/home/ga/Documents/rockets/stability_check.ork'. The initial unstable fin height is artificially set to 0.015m (1.5cm). To achieve stability, the fin height must be increased to at least 0.050m (5.0cm). The agent must run a simulation to ensure it is 'up to date' and create a text report at '/home/ga/Documents/exports/stability_report.txt'.
